prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. The 2023 U-17 Africa Cup of Nations, also known as the 2023 U-17 AFCON, is the 15th edition of the biennial international youth football tournament organized by the Confederation of African Football (CAF), featuring male national teams aged 17 and under. It was decided in May 2021 that Algeria would host the tournament, which would have expanded to twelve teams instead of eight. The top four teams would qualify as CAF representatives for the 2023 FIFA U-17 World Cup, and Cameroon would be the defending champions, as they participate in the 2019 .
B. The 2023 U-17 Africa Cup of Nations, also referred to as 2023 U17 AFCON for brevity, or as the TotalEnergies U-17 Africa Cup of Nations when considering sponsorship, represents the fifteenth iteration of this competition. If previous tournaments lacking hosts are factored in, it would be the twentieth. This biennial international youth football event is orchestrated by the Confederation of African Football (CAF) and features male national teams comprised of athletes who are 17 years old or younger. It was determined in May 2021 that Algeria would serve as the host nation for this particular tournament. This specific edition was slated to be the second instance where the tournament's format had been enlarged to accommodate twelve teams, a change from the previous eight-team structure. The four leading teams from this championship will secure berths in the 2023 FIFA U-17 World Cup, thereby acting as the CAF's designated representatives. Cameroon currently holds the title of reigning champions, having emerged victorious in the 2019 edition of the tournament.
C. The 2023 U-17 Africa Cup of Nations known as 2023 U17 AFCON for short and/or the TotalEnergies U-17 Africa Cup of Nations for sponsorship purposes is the 15th edition (20th if editions of the tournament without hosts are included) of the biennial international youth football tournament organized by the Confederation of African Football (CAF) its male national teams consisting of players aged 17 and below. In May 2021, it was decided that the tournament would be hosted by Algeria. This would have been the second edition of the tournament to have expanded to twelve teams instead of eight. The top four teams of the tournament will qualify for the 2023 FIFA U-17 World Cup as the CAF representatives. Cameroon are the defending champions, having won the 2019 edition

Answer:

The top four teams would qualify as CAF representatives for the 2023 FIFA U-17 World Cup, and Cameroon would be the defending champions, as they participate in the 2019 .

If previous tournaments lacking hosts are factored in, it would be the twentieth. It was determined in May 2021 that Algeria would serve as the host nation for this particular tournament. The four leading teams from this championship will secure berths in the 2023 FIFA U-17 World Cup, thereby acting as the CAF's designated representatives. Cameroon currently holds the title of reigning champions, having emerged victorious in the 2019 edition of the tournament.
